summary: Dark/Light Mode UI Testing Complete - All tests passed. The themed-icon CSS classes are working correctly. Footer social media icons (X, Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook) are visible in dark mode with proper filter applied. Dark mode toggle works on both desktop and mobile. Text contrast is good in dark mode (rgb(240, 240, 245) on dark background). Note: The Features.tsx component with themed-icon-primary class is NOT rendered on the home page - the home page uses CoreValueProps.tsx which uses emoji icons instead of SVG icons.
critical_code_review_comments: Features.tsx has themed-icon-primary class on lines 143 and 209, but this component is NOT rendered on the home page. The home page uses CoreValueProps.tsx which uses emoji icons (🔄, 🔗, 📋) instead of SVG icons.
The themed-icon class is only used in HomeFooter/index.tsx for social media icons (X, Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook) - these are working correctly in dark mode.
The CSS in globals.css (lines 190-203) correctly applies filter transformations for dark mode: themed-icon gets brightness/invert filter, themed-icon-primary gets a blue-tinted filter.
MobileNavigationBar/styled.tsx has dark-mode-aware filter on IconButton (lines 81-87) that properly handles theme.palette.mode === 'dark'.
test_credentials: No authentication needed for home page and login page testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Dark/light mode UI is working correctly. The themed-icon CSS classes are properly applied and working. Footer social icons are visible in both modes. The Features.tsx component is not used on the home page - CoreValueProps.tsx is used instead with emoji icons. Login page works in both modes on desktop and mobile.

desktop_tests.footer_icons_dark_mode: PASS - 4 social icons visible with themed-icon class

observations.themed_icon_usage: Only used in footer for social media icons (X, Instagram, LinkedIn, Facebook)
observations.themed_icon_primary_usage: Defined in Features.tsx but component not rendered on home page
observations.core_value_props_icons: Uses emoji icons (🔄, 🔗, 📋) with gradient backgrounds - no themed-icon class needed
observations.dark_mode_toggle_location: Near EN dropdown in header, uses data-testid='DarkModeOutlinedIcon' in light mode and 'LightModeOutlinedIcon' in dark mode
